ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RANDALL WOLF'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS ACTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT SHOWN BY A PREPONDERANCE OF THE EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT HAS SUFFICIENT MINIMUM CONTACTS WITH CALIFORNIA TO SUPPORT PERSONAL JURISDICTION. PLAINTIFF HAS ONLY SHOWED A ONE-TIME PURCHASE OF A MAGIC SHOW AND THREE TRIPS TO CALIFORNIA FOR TRAINING PURPOSES. THIS IS A ONE-TIME TRANSACTION, AND NOT ON GOING BUSINESS DEALINGS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LESLIE C. NICHOLS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Browne of Law Offices of John Browne III telephone number 415-421-6700 for Plaintiffs John Calvert and Tammy Calvert.
Kenneth Sandall of Branson, Brinkop, Griffith & Campo LLP telephone number 650-365-7710 for Defendant Randall Wolf MD.

The Court suggest that the Parties try mediation, meet and confer, and try and settle this case. The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendant Randall Wolf's motion to stay or dismiss action based on inconvenient forum is denied. While either Indiana or Ohio is a suitable forum for this case and there is little relationship between this case and San Francisco, plaintiff Tammy Calvert has shown that it would be a serious hardship which likely would eliminate her ability to pursue her claims if she were required to litigate in Indiana or Ohio. In contrast, Dr. Wolf has shown that he is able to defend this action in California and his concerns that the Magicarama which is the subject of this case is physically located in Indiana can be dealt with by videotaping or photographing Magicarama and his concerns about missing work in Houston are no different if he has to travel to San Francisco or the Cincinnati area for travel. Accordingly, the court exercises its discretion to deny the motion. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss Action
Off Calendar Sep-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOHN R. BROWNE III, TEL. NO. 421-6700 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KENNETH W. SANDALL OF BRANSON, BRINKOP & STRONG LLP, TEL. NO. (650) 365-7710 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT RANDALL WOLF'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss Action is Granted. Plaintiffs have not shown by a preponderance of the evidence that Defendant has sufficient minimum contacts with California to support personal jurisdiction. Plaintiff has only showed a one-time purchase of a magic show and three trips to California for training purposes. This is a one-time transaction, and not on going business dealings. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/LCN)
